backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the accident documentation backend, defining all data types (AccidentReport, VehicleInfo, OtherVehicle, Witness, etc.) and including storage and authorization mixins with data migration support.
backend/migration.mo: Motoko migration module that defines OldAccidentReport and NewAccidentReport types and handles runtime data migration, adding fields like otherVehicle, witnesses, videos, and expanded vehicleInfo to existing records.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (Other Vehicle section, multi-witness section, video uploader, injury photo prominence) to their affected frontend files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Auto-generated summary document describing the purpose and key contents of each frontend source file, serving as a reference for build planning and code navigation.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for the design system's color tokens in both light and dark modes.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app titled 'Legal Accident Documentation App', mounting the app at the #root div and loading the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with five routes (/, /reports, /reports/$reportId, /fault-reference, /grid) and wraps the app in a QueryClientProvider.
frontend/src/components/AccidentReportFo: A multi-section React form component for creating accident reports, capturing vehicle details, photos, videos, other vehicles, witnesses, and environmental conditions, then submitting via a `useCreateReport` hook.
frontend/src/components/ClaimSummaryPane: A collapsible panel that displays AI-generated incident analysis results (crash type, severity, correlations) for a claim, or prompts the user to upload photos if no analysis exists yet.
frontend/src/components/ContributoryNegl: A collapsible panel presenting UK contributory negligence law, showing fault-split scenarios with visual percentage bars and relevant legal precedents sourced from `negligenceData`.
frontend/src/components/DiscrepancyAlert: Renders a destructive alert when a red-light violation discrepancy is detected between witness testimony and AI-detected traffic signal state, prompting manual adjuster review.
frontend/src/components/FaultMatrixPanel: A collapsible panel that lists fault-matrix scenarios with expandable rows and visual fault-percentage bars, highlighting the scenario that matches a given violation type.
frontend/src/components/FaultReferenceDi: Displays a detailed fault reference card for a given scenario key, including party fault percentage bars, a stacked visual bar, and references to Highway Code, RTA 1988, and case law.
frontend/src/components/GridCellDetail.t: Renders a detailed panel for a fault matrix grid cell, showing fault percentage breakdowns between two vehicles with visual bars, stacked bar, and scenario references including legal/insurer context.
frontend/src/components/InjuryAnalysisPa: Collapsible panel that orchestrates a multi-step injury analysis workflow: photo upload, AI multimodal analysis, backend storage, and export, using mutation hooks and managing step/state transitions.
frontend/src/components/InjuryAnalysisUI: UI component for selecting injured body regions via checkboxes and displaying correlated crash type cards with severity badges and biomechanics references based on the selected regions.
frontend/src/components/InjuryExportButt: Button component that fetches insurance export data for a report and triggers a formatted plain-text file download summarizing injury locations, crash mechanisms, photo records, and disclaimers.
frontend/src/components/InjuryPhotoUploa: File upload component supporting multiple image selections with thumbnail previews, removal, and conversion of selected files to ExternalBlob objects passed via callback.
frontend/src/components/InjuryStorageCon: Presents Store and Dispose action buttons for an injury record, showing a loading state during storage and a success confirmation message once the record is stored.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Defines the main app layout with a sticky header containing navigation buttons (New Report, My Reports, Fault Reference, Grid View), a content outlet, and a footer with branding.
frontend/src/components/LegalReferencePa: Collapsible panel that displays relevant Highway Code rules, Road Traffic Act 1988 sections, and case law entries based on the violations passed as props.
frontend/src/components/LiabilityDisplay: Card component that visualizes fault liability percentages for two parties using a proportional color-coded bar and a detailed percentage breakdown grid.
frontend/src/components/NextStepsPanel.t: Collapsible panel that presents post-accident guidance organized into categorized accordion sections (immediate actions, reporting, claims, legal options, contacts) sourced from a static data file.
frontend/src/components/PhotoGallery.tsx: Displays uploaded accident photos in a responsive grid with hover zoom effect, and opens a navigable lightbox dialog when a photo is clicked.
frontend/src/components/PhotoUpload.tsx: Photo upload component that accepts JPEG/PNG/WebP files up to a configurable maximum, converts them to ExternalBlob instances, shows previews in a grid, and notifies the parent of changes.
frontend/src/components/ReportDetail.tsx: Renders a detailed view of a single accident report, including liability, violations, traffic signs, photos, AI analysis, legal references, and next steps panels.
frontend/src/components/ReportList.tsx: Displays a sorted, paginated list of all accident reports with thumbnails, fault badges, and navigation to individual report detail pages.
frontend/src/components/ScenarioSelector: Renders a grid of selectable accident scenario cards (e.g., rear-end, red-light) with icons and descriptions, emitting the selected scenario key on change.
frontend/src/components/SpeedDisplay.tsx: Displays calculated vehicle velocity in both m/s and mph with color-coded severity styling and a descriptive speed label.
frontend/src/components/TrafficSignsDisp: Renders a list of detected traffic signs from accident photos, showing sign type, position, timestamp, and a simulated confidence badge.
frontend/src/components/ViolationsDispla: Shows detected traffic violations (or a no-violation state) with icons, descriptions, and timestamps in a styled card layout.
frontend/src/data/faultMatrix.ts: Defines a static fault matrix dataset mapping accident scenarios (e.g., rear-end, red-light, lane change) to percentage fault splits, contributing factors, rationale, and related violation types.
frontend/src/data/injuryCorrelation.ts: Defines TypeScript types and static data mapping body regions to crash types, including severity and reference information, based on established crash biomechanics sources like NHTSA and AAAM.
frontend/src/data/legalReferences.ts: Contains static UK legal reference data mapping traffic violation types to relevant Highway Code rules, Road Traffic Act 1988 sections, and case law entries.
frontend/src/data/negligenceData.ts: Provides static UK contributory negligence framework data including fault-split scenarios with percentage breakdowns and legal precedents under the Law Reform (Contributory Negligence) Act 1945.
frontend/src/data/nextSteps.ts: Defines categorised post-incident guidance steps (immediate actions, reporting obligations, claims process) with legal references, structured as static data for display in the UI.
frontend/src/data/scenarioReferences.ts: Contains static reference data for common road collision scenarios, each with Highway Code rules, RTA 1988 sections, case law, and fault percentage breakdowns between parties.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A React hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, reinitialising and invalidating dependent queries when the user's identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication on the ICP platform, managing login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating accident reports and user profiles via the backend actor, including report creation with photo/video metadata.
frontend/src/hooks/useVelocityCalculatio: A small hook that computes velocity in m/s and mph from distance and time inputs using memoized calculations.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that sets up Tailwind directives and defines light/dark mode CSS custom properties for the design system, including fault reference tool tokens.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/BirdsEyeGridPage.tsx: Renders an interactive grid page showing fault percentage breakdowns between multiple vehicles across accident scenarios, with dialogs for detailed cell information.
frontend/src/pages/FaultReferencePage.ts: A page for selecting accident scenarios and displaying relevant UK legal references, Highway Code rules, case law, and indicative fault percentages.
frontend/src/pages/NewReportPage.tsx: A simple page wrapper that renders the AccidentReportForm component for creating a new accident report.
frontend/src/pages/ReportDetailPage.tsx: Fetches and displays a single accident report by ID from the route params, showing loading skeletons and an error state if not found.
frontend/src/pages/ReportsPage.tsx: A simple page wrapper that renders the ReportList component for viewing all saved accident reports.
frontend/src/utils/speedColorCoding.ts: Utility functions that return Tailwind CSS color classes and descriptive labels based on speed thresholds (low, medium, high).
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration with dark mode, custom OKLCH-based color tokens, typography/container-queries/animate plugins, and extended theme settings for a component-rich UI.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state for an auto/personal injury claim submission app, listing existing features (other vehicle section, witness entries, video uploader, injury photo uploader placement) and pending new feature requests.
